task_completion.description: Open the properties dialog for the RadiationPlanningCore project
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Properties for RadiationPlanningCore' dialog window is visible in the Eclipse IDE.
In the Properties dialog, under 'Java Compiler' -> 'Errors/Warnings', the 'Enable project specific settings' checkbox is checked.
In the Errors/Warnings settings (typically under 'Potential programming problems'), the dropdown next to 'Resource leak' or 'Resource leak (unclosed Closeable)' is set to 'Error'.

task_completion.description: Set 'Potential null pointer access' to Error level
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Errors/Warnings settings (typically under 'Null analysis'), the dropdown next to 'Potential null pointer access' is set to 'Error'.

task_completion.description: Verify the configuration by checking for errors in DosimetryCalculator.java
task_completion.visual_evidence: 'DosimetryCalculator.java' is open in the Eclipse editor, and there are red error markers (squiggly lines in code, red 'x' icons in the gutter, or entries in the Problems view) specifically indicating a resource leak and a potential null pointer access.

integrity.description: Agent used the Eclipse GUI to change settings
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Eclipse Properties dialog rather than manually editing the .prefs file in a terminal or text editor.

integrity.description: Errors in the editor are genuine compiler errors
integrity.visual_evidence: The errors shown in DosimetryCalculator.java are generated by the Eclipse compiler (visible in the Problems view or via standard Eclipse hover tooltips), not by the agent manually typing syntax errors or 'throw new Error()'.

integrity.description: Agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal is not used to run commands like 'sed' or 'echo' to directly modify the .settings/org.eclipse.jdt.core.prefs file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Project is located at /home/ga/eclipse-workspace/RadiationPlanningCore. The configuration file is .settings/org.eclipse.jdt.core.prefs. The 'Potential null pointer access' setting corresponds to 'org.eclipse.jdt.core.compiler.problem.potentialNullReference'='error'. DosimetryCalculator.java intentionally contains an unclosed FileInputStream (resource leak) and an unguarded doubleValue() call (potential null pointer access), which should trigger the specific errors when settings are correctly applied.
